interpretation: AUC = 0.54 indicates MTM-L alone has limited discriminative power on this corpus. Brier score = 0.231 (lower is better; baseline of always-predicting-mean ≈ 0.00). 87% of laws scored MTM-L = 0, reflecting that most struck-down Canadian statutes are NOT literally misaligned on face — they fail at the purposive/contextual level (MTM-F, MTM-C). Implication: MTM-L is a conservative, narrow test. It flags textually-broken statutes, but many Charter-invalid laws pass MTM-L because their pathology is purposive, not literal. The MTM-F and MTM-C tiers (coming soon) are where the remaining predictive signal should live.
